name: Ahaz of Judah
notes: The reign of the wicked king Ahaz of Judah, told in 2 Kings 16 and again in 2 Chronicles 28. Both accounts share a core frame: his age and sixteen-year reign, his refusal to do right like David his father, his walking in the way of the kings of Israel, his burning of his son(s) in the fire after the abominations of the heathen, his sacrificing in the high places and under every green tree, the war brought by Rezin of Syria and Pekah of Israel, his appeal to Assyria with the silver and gold of the house of the LORD, and his death and burial with Hezekiah reigning in his stead. Each writer keeps his own vantage: Kings dwells on the altar of Damascus and the despoiling of the temple furniture (16:10-18), while Chronicles enlarges on the great slaughter in Judah, the prophet Oded and the captives sent home, the inroads of Edom and the Philistines, and Ahaz's further trespass with the gods of Damascus (28:5-15,17-25). These are complementary portraits of one king, the same record viewed from the throne and from the temple.

links.label: Twenty years old ... reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem ... did not that which was right in the sight of the LORD ... like David his father
he walked in the way of the kings of Israel
to pass through the fire according to the abominations of the heathen whom the LORD cast out from before the children of Israel
burnt incense in the high places and on the hills and under every green tree
Rezin king of Syria ... came up to Jerusalem to war
Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel
Ahaz sent ... to ... king of Assyria
the silver and gold that was found in the house of the LORD ... sent it ... to the king of Assyria
the rest of ... his ... acts ... written in the book of the ... kings of Judah
Ahaz slept with his fathers ... and Hezekiah his son reigned in his stead
